what_it_does: A JSON interpretive ledger for the PROGRESS.md tracker: it records what the markdown tracker does, lists observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, themes, and embedding summaries, and declares no code symbols.
observations: It identifies itself as an interpretive layer for the claude-ds analysis/PROGRESS.md file.

It contains structured fields: project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, intention_for_embedding.
It summarizes PROGRESS.md as a resumable tracker with a 12-folder status table and a chronological session log.

It records specific findings: zoning token #fdfcf7 promoted to CONFIRMED, gold ramp hexes, ratio independent of density and LOD, a register/purpose dimension, and unification of parallel archetype lists.
It notes that the status table is hand-maintained prose/checkmarks and therefore drift-prone.
It reports differing sampled ramp hexes across sessions without reconciling them here.
Its symbols array is empty, consistent with a non-code interpretive file.
It includes embedding-ready summary and intention sentences.
standouts: The interpretive layer treats a progress tracker as a memory substrate rather than a simple checklist.
It captures axis orthogonality (ratio, density, LOD, register) as a resolved analytical finding.
It records an anti-drift unification that welded three parallel archetype lists into one catalogue.
It flags a data-quality issue: conflicting sampled gold-ramp hexes between sessions s3 and s6.
conventions: JSON interpretive schema with neutral, file-local annotations.
Append-newest narrative log alongside a structured status table.
concerns: The JSON is a second-order interpretation of a tracker, so any drift in the hand-maintained markdown propagates here.
It reports conflicting sampled colour values without resolving them.
All 12 table rows share the same analysed status, making the status ladder underutilized.
It references sibling docs (e.g., SYNTHESIS-PLAN, FINDINGS-LOG) that are not verifiable from this file alone.
notes: A neutral, file-level interpretation of the PROGRESS.md tracker, useful as a resumable index but dependent on the accuracy of the hand-maintained source tracker.
questions: Which sampled ramp hexes are canonical given the s3 and s6 discrepancy?
Is the tracker generated from sibling docs or maintained manually?
How is the status ladder intended to be used if only 'analysed' appears in the table?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the JSON presents itself as an interpretive layer summarizing the tracker's structure, findings, and role, and it does exactly that.
intention_signals: Inferred: to turn a long-running, hand-maintained progress tracker into a machine-readable memory substrate so a fresh agent can quickly grasp what has been analysed, what was confirmed, and what unification work followed, without re-reading all sibling docs.
novelty: Distinct in that it is a meta-interpretation of a tracker: it does not track progress itself but documents how the tracker functions, what findings it memorializes, and how it resists drift, making the tracker itself a design-substrate artifact.
contribution: Contributes a neutral, file-local interpretive record of the PROGRESS.md tracker, preserving its structure, key findings, concerns, and intended resumability role for fusion queries.
summary_for_embedding: JSON interpretive ledger for the claude-ds PROGRESS.md tracker, summarizing its 12-folder status table, session-log findings, orthogonal axes, unification history, concerns, and embedding-ready purpose.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make the PROGRESS.md tracker readable as a machine-interpretable memory substrate, capturing its role, findings, and drift concerns for resumption and fusion.
